candidates.target: SAXPY loop arrays x/y/z and scalar a
candidates.rationale: Profiling/telemetry indicate the hotspot is memory-bandwidth bound (memory_bound_score=0.85) with low arithmetic intensity; the retrieved pattern recommends replacing 64-bit doubles with smaller satisfactory types to reduce cache pressure and memory traffic, improving cache behavior and throughput.
candidates.action_sketch: Assess required numeric precision; if acceptable, change x/y/z from double to float (and a to float), update initialization constants with f-suffix, keep alignment, and update checksum to accumulate in double (or uint64) from float inputs to preserve a stable check.
preconditions: Application accuracy requirements allow single-precision (or mixed precision) for x/y/z without violating output tolerances
Compiler flags keep vectorization enabled (e.g., -O3) and no strict FP mode changes unless explicitly approved
correctness_checks: Compare float vs double outputs with relative/absolute tolerance (e.g., max_abs_err and max_rel_err over z) for representative n and reps
Verify checksum (or a higher-quality reference reduction) matches expected within tolerance across runs
rollback_criteria: If numerical error exceeds agreed tolerance or checksum/reference comparison fails, revert arrays and computations to double
If runtime does not improve measurably (e.g., <3-5%) or regressions appear in cache/memory metrics, revert
